title: dianthus caryophyllus subsp. garganicus
intro: Dianthus caryophyllus subsp. garganicus, also known as Gargano carnation, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, traditionally used to treat various ailments, including digestive issues, headaches, and colds. The plant's health benefits include its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and promote wound healing. Dianthus caryophyllus subsp. garganicus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various health issues.
